dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3590 IS: Water Infrastructure Resilience and Sustainability Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Blunt Rochester (for herself and Mr. Curtis) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works

form.official-title: To amend the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize the clean water infrastructure resiliency and sustainability program, to amend the Safe Drinking Water Act to reauthorize certain resilience and sustainability programs, and for other purposes.

subsection.text: This Act may be cited as the Water Infrastructure Resilience and Sustainability Act of 2026.
The table of contents for this Act is as follows:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 1. Short title; table of contents.
Sec. 101. Reauthorization of clean water infrastructure resiliency and sustainability program.
Sec. 201. Reauthorization of drinking water infrastructure resilience and sustainability.
Sec. 202. Reauthorization of midsize and large drinking water system infrastructure resilience and sustainability program.

section.header: Reauthorization of clean water infrastructure resiliency and sustainability program
section.text: Section 223(g)(1) of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act (33 U.S.C. 1302a(g)(1)) is amended by striking fiscal years 2022 through 2026 and inserting fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

section.header: Reauthorization of drinking water infrastructure resilience and sustainability
section.text: Section 1459A(l) of the Safe Drinking Water Act (42 U.S.C. 300j–19a(l)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2), by striking fiscal years 2022 through 2026 and inserting fiscal years 2027 through 2031; and
in paragraph (6), by striking fiscal years 2022 through 2026 and inserting fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

section.header: Reauthorization of midsize and large drinking water system infrastructure resilience and sustainability program
section.text: Section 1459F(f)(1) of the Safe Drinking Water Act (42 U.S.C. 300j–19g(f)(1)) is amended by striking fiscal years 2022 through 2026 and inserting fiscal years 2027 through 2031.
